title: How come the entry plugs parachutes were never used
display_title: How come the entry plugs parachutes were never used
wikitext:
[]: How come the entry plugs' parachutes were never used?             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Jan 10, 2011 10:25 pm So, I was looking for some images of the Angels and I found an Artbook... As we can see in exhibit A there actualy IS a parachute on the Entry Plugs! How comes we never saw it? Exhibit A:

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 10, 2011 10:39 pm What you see in the production materials isn't necessarily what you seen in the final product. Rei was originally named Yui Ichijo. Touji was supposed to appear legless in The End of Evangelion. And Kaworu was supposed to appear several episodes earlier than he did. But as things progress, things were changed, things were left untouched, and other things were added in. This is true of a lot of creative works. It's also very possible that the parachute system was never meant to be used, but Yamashita designed it because he wanted the device to be practical and functional in a variety of capacities.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jan 10, 2011 10:43 pm Also, I can only think of one instance of the Entry Plug being successfully ejected: Episode 5. Under the circumstances, it seemed that the Plug wasn't functioning correctly. That's why it never worked.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Jan 10, 2011 10:43 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Yamashita designed it because he wanted the device to be practical and functional in a variety of capacities.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Jan 11, 2011 12:06 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jan 11, 2011 11:51 am That, and simply because something is designed for the show doesn't actually mean it gets to be in the show. Live Eva Monkey said, there are a lot of things dreamed up for the show that doesn't actually get green-lit and produced. Parachutes, I guess, were just one of those many things left on the creative room floor. Now as to why they were not green-lit for the shows, I have no clue.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Tue Jan 11, 2011 11:52 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: A parachute in ep 5, if it had deployed, wouldn't have been of use anyway. One needs more distance and space for them to unfold; she was shot right up into the ceiling and didn't have far enough to fall.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 11, 2011 1:03 pm arael wrote: I was wondering why not in episode 5 when we see Rei smashing in the Test room after the forced ejection out of Eva 00...

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Tue Jan 11, 2011 2:21 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Maybe because parachutes can't properly deploy unless they're traveling at a certain speed, height, and angle?

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Jan 11, 2011 3:15 pm I have the Newtype 100% collection artbook and I didn´t even notice those parachutes! Damn. But having parachutes seems a little pointless now, seeing how every attempt to eject the entry plug always happens indoors, gets rejected by the Eva or is obstructed by random outside factors.

[]:             Quote Post by The Forgotten Knight » Sun Jan 16, 2011 3:29 am Because it's more dramatic to fall like a rock than to land gracefully.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sun Jan 16, 2011 1:55 pm The Forgotten Knight wrote: Because it's more dramatic to fall like a rock than to land gracefully.
